theme: Set tolls so selfish drivers behave socially
title: Corridor Tolls: Steering Selfish Drivers to the Social Optimum
strategies: do-nothing: charge no tolls and accept the untolled selfish (Wardrop) equilibrium -- reproduces the evaluator's do-nothing baseline (~0.1)

congestion-proportional toll (the trap): run the untolled equilibrium, then toll each route by the congestion delay it currently shows, tau_e = a_e*f_e^p_e; this drops the marginal factor p_e and, on routes with mixed exponents, under-tolls the steep routes and steers flow the wrong way

marginal-cost internalisation (the insight): solve the social optimum at the representative demand and price each route at its marginal externality f_e*l_e'(f_e)=a_e*p_e*f_e^p_e, evaluated at the social-optimum flow (not the untolled flow), carrying the p_e factor

distribution-robust tolling: search over the fixed toll vector to minimise the average travel time across the sampled demand scenarios, fitting the whole demand spread rather than a single representative demand -- beats the single-point marginal toll and approaches (but cannot reach) the per-scenario ideal
